label.purpose: Eyebrows, buttons, metadata tags. Always uppercase, wide-tracked.

pullquote.purpose: Direct sourced quotes from Falls or critics, always italic serif.

shadows.name: hover-lift-rest
shadows.purpose: Resting shadow on cards that are interactive but not yet hovered.

shadows.name: hover-lift-active
shadows.purpose: Card shadow on hover, paired with a 4px upward translate.

shadows.name: pill-shell
shadows.purpose: Soft shadow under small floating UI shells like filter-pill containers.

motion.name: color-shift
motion.value: color 200ms ease, border-color 200ms ease, background-color 200ms ease
motion.purpose: Default hover transition for links, buttons, and borders.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action button: oxblood fill, cream uppercase label, square corners.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: #6e1423; color: #f1e7d3; font-family: 'Big Shoulders Display', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; font-size: 0.875rem; padding: 14px 28px; border: none; border-radius: 0; transition: background 200ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #9a2235; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #c9a227; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary button: transparent with a hairline border, used for lower-emphasis CTAs.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: transparent; color: #171210; font-family: 'Big Shoulders Display', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; font-size: 0.875rem; padding: 14px 28px; border: 1px solid rgba(23,18,16,0.3); border-radius: 0; transition: color 200ms ease, border-color 200ms ease; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { color: #6e1423; border-color: #6e1423; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #c9a227; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Production Card
components.description: Thumbnail card used for production grids: flat at rest, lifts with a warm shadow on hover.
components.html:
King Lear
Goodman Theatre · 2006
components.css: .ds-card { display: block; text-decoration: none; } .ds-card-media { aspect-ratio: 3/4; background: #171210; overflow: hidden; margin-bottom: 12px; } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Big Shoulders Display', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; color: #171210; line-height: 1.2; transition: color 200ms ease; } .ds-card:hover .ds-card-title { color: #6e1423; } .ds-card-meta { font-family: 'Big Shoulders Display', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.625rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.16em; color: rgba(23,18,16,0.45); margin-top: 4px; }

components.name: Filter Pill
components.description: Small rounded toggle used for timeline/archive filters; the one place radius is allowed.
components.css: .ds-pill { border-radius: 9999px; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: 'Big Shoulders Display', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.18em; border: 1px solid transparent; color: rgba(23,18,16,0.65); background: transparent; transition: background 200ms ease, color 200ms ease; } .ds-pill-active, .ds-pill[aria-pressed='true'] { background: #6e1423; border-color: #6e1423; color: #f1e7d3; }

components.name: Section Eyebrow
components.description: Small uppercase oxblood (or gold, on dark sections) label that precedes every section heading.
components.css: .ds-eyebrow { color: #6e1423; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.28em; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 16px; }

narrative.overview: This system is built to feel like the printed program you're handed walking into a major regional theater: heavy cream stock, oxblood and gold ink, a serif pulled from a body that's been setting type for centuries, and a display face with the blunt, condensed weight of a marquee. It is not a marketing site pretending to be a portfolio, and it is not a memorial pretending to be an archive. Every choice should read as institutional confidence: the kind of restraint that comes from not needing to perform enthusiasm, because the work already speaks. The system explicitly rejects the generic actor/director portfolio template and the corporate SaaS landing page. Surfaces are flat and square-cornered by default; elevation is earned only through interaction, never decoration.
keyCharacteristics: Flat, square-cornered surfaces; shadow only appears in response to hover, never at rest.
A strict three-color discipline: cream/ink for structure, oxblood as the one accent, gold for labels and lit states.
Display type set in heavy, condensed, almost-too-large sizes; body and quotes set in an italic literary serif.
A fine film-grain texture layered over dark sections, never over light ones.
Authority through specificity: real titles, real names, real years, real numbers.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Oxblood is the only saturated color carrying meaning. Gold marks labels and lit states, oxblood marks action and identity.

rules.name: The Paper-and-Ink Rule
rules.body: At least 80% of any given screen is cream or ink. A third surface color should be a tint of one of those two.

rules.name: The Marquee Rule
rules.body: Display type is reserved for the hero headline and section headings only, never body copy or captions.

rules.name: The Earned-Elevation Rule
rules.body: Nothing has a shadow at rest except small floating UI shells. Cards and sections are flat until proven interactive.

dos: Do lead every section with a real number, title, name, or date before any adjective.
Do keep all card and button corners perfectly square; the one radius exception is small pill-shaped controls.
Do reserve display type for hero headlines and section headings only.
Do treat the oxblood/cream/ink/gold palette as closed.
Do let real photography carry visual weight; grain texture is only for dark sections without a photo behind them.
donts: Don't build a generic actor/director portfolio template.
Don't build a corporate SaaS landing page with hero-metric tiles, gradient text, or repeated icon-card grids.
Don't frame any section, including the Goodman tenure, as a retirement retrospective or memorial.
Don't add resting shadows to cards or sections.
Don't round corners on anything except small pill-shaped controls.
Don't use side-stripe colored borders anywhere.
